ideaItems.title: new loop process add a post deploy recovery telemetry review checkpoint
ideaItems.body: New loop process — Add a post-deploy recovery telemetry review checkpoint Observed 1 times in pattern reflection output. Recurred 2 times in the active detector window. Route this validated self-evolving candidate back through the canonical workflow.

ideaItems.title: BRIK gmail pipeline — In-Progress recovery not auto-scheduled
ideaItems.body: When an ops-inbox session crashes mid-processing, the email retains the In-Progress label (Brikette/Queue/In-Progress). Because In-Progress is in TERMINAL_LABELS, gmail_organize_inbox will never re-queue it. The email silently disappears from the active queue until gmail_reconcile_in_progress is run manually. Operators are unlikely to remember to run this tool, so stuck emails accumulate over time and customer messages go unanswered.

ideaItems.title: BRIK gmail pipeline — inquiry draft dedup missing at tool level
ideaItems.body: The booking reservation path has checkBookingRefDuplicate() but the customer inquiry path (gmail_create_draft called from ops-inbox) has no equivalent guard. A session crash between draft creation and gmail_mark_processed leaves the email with no terminal label, so the next gmail_organize_inbox run re-queues it. The operator then processes it again, producing a second draft in Gmail for the same customer email.

ideaItems.title: BRIK gmail pipeline — orphaned label accumulation no cleanup
ideaItems.body: ensureLabelMap creates Gmail labels on demand but never removes stale or renamed ones. The LEGACY_LABELS map handles reading old label names during schema transitions but the old labels remain in the Gmail account indefinitely. Over time the Brikette label hierarchy becomes cluttered with orphaned labels from past naming schemes, making manual Gmail navigation harder and creating ambiguity about which label represents the current state.

ideaItems.title: BRIK email pipeline — intent extraction collapses clause-level questions, double-counts intents
ideaItems.body: Mixed-intent guest emails (e.g. availability + cancellation policy in one message) currently collapse to a single dominant answer path, leaving one sub-question unaddressed. The extraction layer must atomize compound clauses before scenario ranking to prevent routine pre-booking emails from dropping a question.

ideaItems.title: BRIK email pipeline — quality gate advisory-only; coverage scoring lexical-only
ideaItems.body: The quality gate being advisory at the tool boundary means a failed draft can still be submitted as a Gmail draft if the operator misses the quality.passed flag — the safety guarantee only exists in a workflow doc, not enforced in code. Separately, the lexical-only coverage scorer generates false negatives on valid answers that paraphrase rather than echo question keywords, creating unnecessary operator patch churn.

ideaItems.title: BRIK email pipeline — template ranker accepts weak matches, booking_monitor triggers too broadly
ideaItems.body: Template-ranker always selects candidate 0 from BM25 results even when the match is below any useful similarity threshold, producing confident-sounding but wrong answers for unknown topics. Separately, booking_monitor activates on any mention of 'booking' rather than only on operational booking actions, causing quality checks to demand reference links on ordinary FAQ drafts and generating false failures.

ideaItems.title: XA local catalog pipeline — sync/build broken without cloud
ideaItems.body: The xa-uploader to xa-b data pipeline is broken locally — sync errors on missing contract, build has no local fallback, CSV is empty. Decoupling data from images enables local dev workflow.

ideaItems.title: BRIK brikette app — useGuideSearch buildIndex performs per-guide t() calls on every language change
ideaItems.body: Language switches on a guide listing page trigger a full index rebuild with per-entry translation calls. If i18next lookup is not O(1) cached, this is measurable jank on slow devices. Needs investigation before deciding the fix approach.

ideaItems.title: BRIK brikette app — flat label prop signatures on BookingCalendarPanel, SecureBookingPageClient, OctorateCustomPageShell
ideaItems.body: 14–17 flat label props per component makes callsites verbose and fragile — adding a new label anywhere requires touching every caller. Grouped label objects are a one-time migration that scales as the booking flow grows.

ideaItems.title: BRIK brikette app — createGuideLabelReader + buildBreadcrumb copy-pasted across 4 how-to-get-here route modules
ideaItems.body: Four near-identical route modules with copy-pasted 30-line functions means a bug in the label reader or breadcrumb builder has been silently present in all routes since the last edit. A factory eliminates this propagation risk before a fifth route is added.

ideaItems.title: BRIK brikette app — resolveTranslatedCopy i18n helper duplicated across 4 modules
ideaItems.body: Four-way duplication of a core i18n validation helper means any future change to the sentinel pattern must be applied in four places with no mechanism to keep them in sync. A single shared export eliminates that maintenance risk.

ideaItems.title: BRIK reception — no shared app-wide layout standard
ideaItems.body: Staff navigate between bar, inbox, and other screens with no visual continuity — each screen looks like a different app. A shared layout standard would reduce cognitive load and reinforce the reception brand.

ideaItems.title: ai to mechanistic schedule gmail in progress reconciliation outside the agent loop
ideaItems.body: AI-to-mechanistic — Schedule Gmail In-Progress reconciliation outside the agent loop Observed 1 times in pattern reflection output. Recurred 2 times in the active detector window. Route this validated self-evolving candidate back through the canonical workflow.

ideaItems.title: new standing data source add a standing ops feed for inbox recovery outcomes
ideaItems.body: New standing data source — Add a standing ops feed for inbox recovery outcomes Observed 1 times in pattern reflection output. Recurred 2 times in the active detector window. Route this validated self-evolving candidate back through the canonical workflow.

ideaItems.title: BRIK reception inbox — draft edit diff not persisted for learning
ideaItems.body: Staff edits to AI-generated drafts are a direct quality signal that is currently discarded. Capturing and feeding back edit patterns would improve future draft generation.

ideaItems.title: xa-uploader sync panel — flat layout, native unstyled checkboxes
ideaItems.body: xa-uploader UI review found the sync panel uses a flat list with no grouping and native unstyled checkboxes, making it inconsistent with the rest of the gate-* design-system UI and harder for operators to orient quickly to what they need to confirm before publishing.

ideaItems.title: XA xa-uploader — Edit mode needs progressive filter-down product selection
ideaItems.body: Edit mode product selection requires browsing a flat list — should use the same progressive brand/collection/size cascade as Add mode, auto-selecting when filter narrows to one product.

ideaItems.title: BOS core workflow skills — enforce size budgets
ideaItems.body: The triad that every build cycle depends on has already regressed significantly; without guardrails it will drift back into monolith state.

ideaItems.title: XA xa-b department landing — text-only, no visual engagement
ideaItems.body: Department landing pages are the primary browse entry point for each department (women/men/kids). They currently use plain text links with no visual engagement, which fails to convey the luxury brand and provide the visual shopping experience expected of a high-end fashion storefront.

ideaItems.title: XA xa-b empty states — plain text, no visual treatment
ideaItems.body: Empty states are user-facing edge cases that break the luxury brand immersion. The cart empty state and filter no-results use basic text with no visual treatment, creating a stark contrast with the polished product browsing experience.

ideaItems.title: XA xa-b product cards — missing New In badge
ideaItems.body: The site prominently features a New In section but product cards have no visual indicator to distinguish new arrivals when they appear in mixed listings. This reduces the effectiveness of the new-arrivals merchandising strategy.

ideaItems.title: XA catalog sync — no image binary upload in cloud mode
ideaItems.body: Without an actual image upload path, every product synced in cloud mode risks broken images on the storefront. This is the most critical gap in the catalog workflow — the entire visual merchandising layer depends on images resolving correctly. Must stay within Cloudflare free tier.

ideaItems.title: PWRB hardware SKU — no unit selected, CE-marked supplier research needed
PWRB pilot venue shortlist — no venues selected from IPEI customer base
PWRB PSP pre-auth hold — 52h auth window unconfirmed, validation needed
